1	# LedgerMint - Digital Collectibles Exchange
2	
3	A full-stack platform for listing, buying, and managing digital collectibles (NFTs). Features role-based access control, order state machine with idempotency, real-time notifications, analytics dashboards, A/B testing, and anomaly detection.
4	
5	## Architecture
6	
7	| Layer | Technology |
8	|-------|-----------|
9	| Frontend | React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite, React Query, Zustand, Recharts |
10	| Backend | Go 1.22, Echo v4, PostgreSQL 16, JWT auth, AES-256-GCM encryption |
11	| Database | PostgreSQL 16 with advisory locks, CIDR-based IP filtering |
12	| Infrastructure | Docker Compose, Nginx reverse proxy |
13	
14	## Quick Start
15	
16	```bash
17	docker compose up
18	```
19	
20	This single command builds and starts all three services (PostgreSQL, backend API, frontend).
21	
22	## Service URLs and Ports
23	
24	| Service | URL | Port |
25	|---------|-----|------|
26	| Frontend (UI) | http://localhost | 80 |
27	| Backend (API) | http://localhost:8080 | 8080 |
28	| PostgreSQL | localhost | 5432 |
29	
30	The frontend Nginx proxy forwards `/api/*` requests to the backend, so all API calls work through http://localhost/api/ as well.
31	
32	## First-Time Setup
33	
34	No default admin credentials are included in the database migrations. On first deployment, you must bootstrap an administrator account:
35	
36	```bash
37	# 1. Start services
38	docker compose up
39	
40	# 2. Create the initial administrator (interactive)
41	make create-admin
42	
43	# Or use the API directly:
44	curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/setup/admin \
45	  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
46	  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"<STRONG-PASSWORD>","display_name":"System Administrator"}'
47	```
48	
49	The bootstrap endpoint (`POST /api/setup/admin`) is only available when no administrator account exists. It is intentionally CSRF-exempt because it is called before any user session exists and is a one-time bootstrap operation — once an admin account is created, the endpoint returns `409 Conflict` and cannot be used again. All other API endpoints return `503 Service Unavailable` until setup is complete. You can check setup status at any time via `GET /api/setup/status`.
50	
51	> **Security:** Never use predictable passwords (e.g. `admin123`, `password`) in production. Rotate credentials immediately after deployment. Default credentials must never be committed to version control.
52	
53	## Development Seed Data
54	
55	For local development, run `make seed` after startup to populate sample users and collectibles. **These credentials are for development only and must never be used in production.**
56	
57	```bash
58	docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -f /dev/stdin < scripts/seed.sql
59	```
60	
61	The seed script creates an admin and sample users with weak passwords suitable only for local testing.
62	
63	## Verification
64	
65	1. Open http://localhost in a browser
66	2. Complete the initial setup (create an admin account) or run `make seed` for development
67	3. Log in with the admin credentials you created
68	4. You should see the Dashboard with stats cards (Open Orders, Unread Notifications, Roles)
69	5. Navigate to **Catalog** - after seeding, three sample collectibles appear
70	6. Navigate to **Users** (admin sidebar) to manage users and roles
71	7. Navigate to **Analytics** to see funnel, retention, and content performance charts
72	
73	## API Endpoints
74	
75	### Setup
76	- `GET /api/setup/status` - Check whether initial setup is complete (public)
77	- `POST /api/setup/admin` - Bootstrap the first administrator account (public, disabled after first admin created)
78	
79	### Authentication
80	- `POST /api/auth/login` - Login (public, rate limited)
81	- `POST /api/auth/refresh` - Refresh tokens (public)
82	- `POST /api/auth/logout` - Logout (authenticated)
83	
84	### Dashboard
85	- `GET /api/dashboard` - User dashboard stats
86	
87	### Users (administrator only)
88	- `POST /api/users` - Create user
89	- `GET /api/users` - List users (paginated)
90	- `GET /api/users/:id` - Get user
91	- `PATCH /api/users/:id` - Update user
92	- `POST /api/users/:id/roles` - Add role
93	- `DELETE /api/users/:id/roles/:roleId` - Remove role
94	- `POST /api/users/:id/unlock` - Unlock account
95	
96	### Collectibles
97	- `GET /api/collectibles` - List published collectibles (paginated)
98	- `GET /api/collectibles/mine` - List seller's own collectibles (seller)
99	- `GET /api/collectibles/:id` - Get collectible with transaction history
100	- `POST /api/collectibles` - Create listing (seller, rate limited)
101	- `PATCH /api/collectibles/:id` - Update listing (seller)
102	- `PATCH /api/collectibles/:id/hide` - Hide listing (administrator)
103	- `PATCH /api/collectibles/:id/publish` - Publish listing (administrator)
104	
105	### Orders
106	- `POST /api/orders` - Create order (buyer, requires Idempotency-Key header)
107	- `GET /api/orders` - List orders (paginated, `?role=buyer|seller`)
108	- `GET /api/orders/:id` - Get order
109	- `POST /api/orders/:id/confirm` - Confirm order (seller)
110	- `POST /api/orders/:id/process` - Start processing (seller)
111	- `POST /api/orders/:id/complete` - Complete order (seller)
112	- `POST /api/orders/:id/cancel` - Cancel order (buyer or seller)
113	- `PATCH /api/orders/:id/fulfillment` - Update fulfillment tracking (seller)
114	
115	### Messages
116	- `GET /api/orders/:orderId/messages` - List messages (paginated)
117	- `POST /api/orders/:orderId/messages` - Send message with optional attachment (multipart, 10MB limit)
118	
119	### Notifications
120	- `GET /api/notifications` - List notifications (paginated, `?unread=true`)
121	- `PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read` - Mark read
122	- `POST /api/notifications/read-all` - Mark all read
123	- `POST /api/notifications/:id/retry` - Retry failed notification
124	- `GET /api/notifications/preferences` - Get preferences
125	- `PUT /api/notifications/preferences` - Update preferences
126	
127	### Analytics (administrator, compliance_analyst)
128	- `GET /api/analytics/funnel` - View-to-order funnel (`?days=7|30`)
129	- `GET /api/analytics/retention` - Retention cohorts (`?days=7|30`)
130	- `GET /api/analytics/content-performance` - Content performance (`?limit=20`)
131	
132	### A/B Tests (administrator)
133	- `POST /api/ab-tests` - Create test
134	- `GET /api/ab-tests` - List tests
135	- `GET /api/ab-tests/:id` - Get test with results
136	- `POST /api/ab-tests/:id/rollback` - Rollback test
137	- `GET /api/ab-tests/assignments` - Get user's variant assignments (all authenticated)
138	
139	### Admin (administrator)
140	- `GET /api/admin/ip-rules` - List IP rules
141	- `POST /api/admin/ip-rules` - Create IP rule
142	- `DELETE /api/admin/ip-rules/:id` - Delete IP rule
143	- `GET /api/admin/anomalies` - List anomaly alerts (administrator, compliance_analyst)
144	- `PATCH /api/admin/anomalies/:id/acknowledge` - Acknowledge anomaly
145	- `GET /api/admin/metrics` - System metrics
146	
147	## Order State Machine
148	
149	```
150	pending -> confirmed -> processing -> completed
151	  |           |
152	  v           v
153	cancelled  cancelled
154	```
155	
156	Valid transitions:
157	- `pending` -> `confirmed` or `cancelled`
158	- `confirmed` -> `processing` or `cancelled`
159	- `processing` -> `completed`
160	
161	## Security Features
162	
163	- **No default credentials** — the first admin must be created via a secure bootstrap endpoint; no passwords are embedded in migrations or committed to version control
164	- JWT authentication via HttpOnly cookies (15-min access, 7-day refresh with rotation)
165	- CSRF protection (double-submit cookie pattern)
166	- AES-256-GCM encryption for PII (emails)
167	- bcrypt password hashing (cost 12)
168	- Account lockout after 5 failed logins (30-minute cooldown)
169	- Per-endpoint rate limiting — user-based **and** IP-based (login, orders, messages, listings)
170	- CIDR-based IP allow/deny rules
171	- PII detection in messages (SSN, phone numbers)
172	- Refresh token family revocation (detects token reuse attacks)
173	- Append-only audit log for auth events, admin actions, order transitions, and moderation
174	- Encrypted keyfile secrets required in non-development environments (staging/production)
175	- Setup guard middleware blocks all API access until initial admin bootstrap is complete
176	
177	## Background Workers
178	
179	| Worker | Interval | Purpose |
180	|--------|----------|---------|
181	| Notification Retry | 60s | Retries failed notifications with exponential backoff |
182	| Anomaly Detector | 5min | Detects excessive cancellations and checkout failures |
183	| A/B Test Evaluator | 5min | Computes conversion rates, auto-rollback if threshold exceeded |
184	| Metrics Writer | 60s | Writes Prometheus-style metrics to log files |
185	| Analytics Rollup | 1h | Pre-aggregates funnel and retention metrics |
186	| Token Cleanup | 1h | Removes expired refresh tokens |
187	
188	## Running Tests
189	
190	```bash
191	./run_tests.sh
192	```
193	
194	This runs both unit tests and API tests. API tests require services to be running (`docker compose up`).
195	
196	- `unit_tests/` - Core logic tests (state transitions, validation, PII detection)
197	- `API_tests/` - Functional API tests (auth, CRUD, permissions, error handling)
198	
199	## Environment Variables
200	
201	| Variable | Default | Description |
202	|----------|---------|-------------|
203	| APP_ENV | (none) | Must be explicitly set to `development` for plaintext env secrets; all other values (including unset) require `SECRETS_KEYFILE` |
204	| DB_PASSWORD | changeme | PostgreSQL password |
205	| JWT_SIGNING_KEY | dev_jwt_secret_change_in_production | JWT signing key (development only) |
206	| AES_MASTER_KEY | (dev key) | 256-bit hex key for AES encryption (development only) |
207	| LISTEN_ADDR | :8080 | Backend listen address |
208	| DATABASE_URL | (auto-composed) | PostgreSQL connection string |
209	| SECRETS_KEYFILE | | Path to AES-256-GCM encrypted keyfile (required when APP_ENV != development) |
210	| SECRETS_PASSPHRASE | | Passphrase to decrypt the keyfile |
211

13	// RequireSetup returns middleware that blocks all API requests (except the
14	// setup endpoints themselves) until at least one administrator account exists.
15	// Once an admin is detected the check is cached so it does not hit the
16	// database on every request.
17	func RequireSetup(userStore *store.UserStore) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
18		var (
19			mu       sync.RWMutex
20			complete bool
21			checked  time.Time
22		)
23	
24		isComplete := func(ctx context.Context) bool {
25			mu.RLock()
26			if complete {
27				mu.RUnlock()
28				return true
29			}
30			// Re-check at most once every 2 seconds to avoid hammering the DB.
31			stale := time.Since(checked) > 2*time.Second
32			mu.RUnlock()
33	
34			if !stale {
35				return false
36			}
37	
38			exists, err := userStore.AdminExists(ctx)
39			if err != nil {
40				return false
41			}
42	
43			mu.Lock()
44			checked = time.Now()
45			if exists {
46				complete = true
47			}
48			mu.Unlock()
49			return exists
50		}
51	
52		return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
53			return func(c echo.Context) error {
54				if isComplete(c.Request().Context()) {
55					return next(c)
56				}
57				return c.JSON(http.StatusServiceUnavailable, map[string]interface{}{
58					"error": map[string]string{
59						"code":    "setup_required",
60						"message": "Initial setup required. Create an administrator account via POST /api/setup/admin before using the API.",
61					},
62				})
63			}
64		}
65	}

11	// This ensures no raw framework errors (HTML or plain text) leak to clients.
12	func GlobalErrorHandler(err error, c echo.Context) {
13		if c.Response().Committed {
14			return
15		}
16	
17		code := http.StatusInternalServerError
18		errCode := dto.CodeInternal
19		message := "internal server error"
20	
21		if he, ok := err.(*echo.HTTPError); ok {
22			code = he.Code
23	
24			// If the error message is already our ErrorResponse format, use it directly
25			if resp, ok := he.Message.(dto.ErrorResponse); ok {
26				c.JSON(code, resp)
27				return
28			}
29	
30			// Map common HTTP status codes to our error codes
31			switch code {
32			case http.StatusBadRequest:
33				errCode = dto.CodeValidation
34				message = "bad request"
35			case http.StatusUnauthorized:
36				errCode = dto.CodeUnauthorized
37				message = "unauthorized"
38			case http.StatusForbidden:
39				errCode = dto.CodeForbidden
40				message = "forbidden"
41			case http.StatusNotFound:
42				errCode = dto.CodeNotFound
43				message = "not found"
44			case http.StatusMethodNotAllowed:
45				errCode = dto.CodeValidation
46				message = "method not allowed"
47			case http.StatusTooManyRequests:
48				errCode = dto.CodeRateLimited
49				message = "rate limited"
50			case http.StatusServiceUnavailable:
51				errCode = dto.CodeInternal
52				message = "service unavailable"
53			}

61		c.JSON(code, dto.ErrorResponse{
62			Error: dto.ErrorDetail{

17	//   - SVG/XML/text: returned as-is (already plaintext)
18	//   - Binary images: extracts printable ASCII runs (catches EXIF metadata,
19	//     embedded comments, and any cleartext strings)
20	func ExtractScannableText(data []byte, mime string, ext string) string {
21		lower := strings.ToLower(ext)
22	
23		// Text and SVG files: content is already scannable.
24		if strings.HasPrefix(mime, "text/") || lower == ".csv" || lower == ".txt" ||
25			mime == "image/svg+xml" || lower == ".svg" {
26			return string(data)
27		}
28	
29		// PDF: extract text from compressed and uncompressed streams.
30		if mime == "application/pdf" || lower == ".pdf" {
31			return extractPDFText(data)
32		}
33	
34		// All other binary formats: extract printable ASCII runs.
35		// This catches EXIF metadata in JPEGs, PNG text chunks, etc.
36		return extractASCIIStrings(data, 8)
37	}

77			decompressed := decompressFlate(streamBytes)
78			if len(decompressed) == 0 {
79				continue
80			}
81	
82			// Extract text from PDF text operators in the decompressed content.
83			matches := pdfTextOpRe.FindAllSubmatch(decompressed, -1)
84			for _, m := range matches {
85				if len(m) >= 2 {
86					parts = append(parts, string(m[1]))

88			}

90			// Also scan the raw decompressed bytes for PII patterns that
91			// might appear outside of formal text operators.

98	// decompressFlate attempts zlib decompression first (standard PDF FlateDecode),
99	// then falls back to raw deflate.
100	func decompressFlate(data []byte) []byte {
101		// Try zlib (PDF standard for FlateDecode).
102		if r, err := zlib.NewReader(bytes.NewReader(data)); err == nil {
103			defer r.Close()
104			out, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(r, 10*1024*1024))
105			if err == nil && len(out) > 0 {
106				return out
107			}
108		}
109	
110		// Fallback: raw deflate.
111		r := flate.NewReader(bytes.NewReader(data))
112		defer r.Close()
113		out, err := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(r, 10*1024*1024))
114		if err == nil {
115			return out
116		}
117		return nil
118	}
119	
120	// extractASCIIStrings extracts contiguous runs of printable ASCII characters
121	// that are at least minLen bytes long. This catches text embedded in EXIF
122	// metadata, PNG tEXt chunks, PDF cleartext, and similar structures.
123	func extractASCIIStrings(data []byte, minLen int) string {
124		var parts []string
125		var current []byte
126	
127		for _, b := range data {
128			if b >= 0x20 && b < 0x7F {
129				current = append(current, b)
130			} else {
131				if len(current) >= minLen {
132					parts = append(parts, string(current))
133				}
134				current = current[:0]
135			}
136		}
137		if len(current) >= minLen {
138			parts = append(parts, string(current))
139		}
140		return strings.Join(parts, " ")
141	}

## Repository Structure and Architecture Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the LedgerMint digital collectibles exchange platform. Here's a comprehensive breakdown:

### 1. **Overall Project Structure**

**Technology Stack:**
- **Framework**: Go 1.22 with Echo v4 web framework
- **Database**: PostgreSQL 16 with advisory locks and pgx driver
- **Frontend**: React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vite, Zustand, React Query
- **Authentication**: JWT via HttpOnly cookies (15-min access tokens, 7-day refresh with rotation)
- **Encryption**: AES-256-GCM for PII, bcrypt for passwords
- **Infrastructure**: Docker Compose, Nginx reverse proxy

**Key Directories:**
- `/backend/cmd/server/` - Entry point (`main.go`)
- `/backend/internal/handler/` - HTTP handlers
- `/backend/internal/service/` - Business logic
- `/backend/internal/store/` - Database access (pgx)
- `/backend/internal/model/` - Domain models
- `/backend/internal/middleware/` - Auth, CSRF, rate limiting, IP filtering
- `/backend/internal/worker/` - Background jobs
- `/backend/internal/router/` - Route definitions
- `/backend/internal/dto/` - Request/response DTOs and error codes
- `/backend/migrations/` - SQL migrations (17 migrations total)

---

### 2. **Message Handling/Messaging Paths**

**Primary Files:**
- `/backend/internal/handler/message_handler.go` - HTTP handlers
- `/backend/internal/service/message_service.go` - Business logic
- `/backend/internal/store/message_store.go` - Database layer
- `/backend/internal/model/message.go` - Domain model

**Key Features:**
- Multipart form-based message submission with optional attachments
- **10MB attachment size limit** (enforced at handler, service, and middleware levels)
- Server-side **PII detection** for message body and attachments
- Attachment storage: **Filesystem-based** (stored in `/app/uploads/{orderId}/{uuid}{ext}`)
- Attachment path never exposed to client; only opaque message UUID used
- **Attachment types validated**: text (plain/CSV), images (JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP), PDF, SVG
- Advanced text extraction for PII scanning across all formats:
  - PDF: FlateDecode stream decompression + text operator extraction
  - Binary images: ASCII string extraction from EXIF/metadata
  - SVG/text: scanned as-is
- Database stores: `attachment_path` (filesystem), `attachment_size`, `attachment_mime`
- Authorization: Only order participants (buyer/seller) can access messages
- Rate limiting: **20 per minute per user**, **40 per minute per IP**

**Message Database Schema** (`messages` table):
```sql
id, order_id, sender_id, body, attachment_path, attachment_size, attachment_mime, created_at
```

**API Endpoints:**
- `GET /api/orders/:orderId/messages` - List messages (paginated)
- `POST /api/orders/:orderId/messages` - Send message with attachment
- `GET /api/messages/:messageId/attachment` - Download attachment by message ID

---

### 3. **Attachment Storage**

**Storage Strategy: Filesystem**
- Path: `/app/uploads/{orderId}/{random-uuid}{original-extension}`
- No database reference for the actual file content
- Database stores only: path, size, MIME type
- Directory created on-demand: `os.MkdirAll(uploadDir, 0o755)`
- File format whitelist enforced both by MIME and extension
- No size tracking in database (calculated from `attachment_size` field)

**PII Detection for Attachments** (`service/text_extract.go`):
- All attachments scanned regardless of format
- Format-specific extraction:
  - **PDF**: Decompresses FlateDecode streams, extracts text from operators `(text) Tj` and `[(text)] TJ`
  - **Binary images**: Extracts contiguous ASCII runs ≥8 chars (catches EXIF, metadata, comments)
  - **SVG/Text**: Scanned as plaintext
- **Regex patterns for PII detection** (`service/pii.go`):
  - SSN: `\b\d{3}-?\d{2}-?\d{4}\b`
  - Phone: `\b(\+?1[-.\s]?)?\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b`
  - Email: `\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+\-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.\-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b`

---

### 4. **Error Handling Strategy**

**Error Codes** (`internal/dto/errors.go`):
```go
ERR_NOT_FOUND           // 404
ERR_FORBIDDEN           // 403
ERR_UNAUTHORIZED        // 401
ERR_CONFLICT            // 409
ERR_RATE_LIMITED        // 429
ERR_VALIDATION          // 422
ERR_ACCOUNT_LOCKED      // 423
ERR_INVALID_CREDENTIALS // 401
ERR_ATTACHMENT_TOO_LARGE // 413
ERR_ORDER_DUPLICATE     // 409
ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION  // 422
ERR_OVERSOLD            // 409
ERR_INTERNAL            // 500
```

**Special Error Code: `setup_required`** (middleware/setup.go)
- Returned as `code: "setup_required"` in response when no admin exists
- HTTP status: **503 Service Unavailable**
- Cached for 2 seconds per request to avoid database hammering
- Format:
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "setup_required",
    "message": "Initial setup required. Create an administrator account via POST /api/setup/admin..."
  }
}
```

**Centralized Error Handler** (`middleware/errorhandler.go`):
- All errors normalized to JSON format with RequestID
- No raw HTML/text errors leak to clients
- Maps HTTP status codes to error codes

---

### 5. **Validation Patterns**

**Validation Framework:** `github.com/go-playground/validator/v10`

**DTO Validation Tags** (`internal/dto/request.go`):

| Field | Rules | Example |
|-------|-------|---------|
| Username | `required,min=3,max=100` | UserCreate, Login |
| Password | `required,min=8,max=128` (bootstrap: `min=12`) | UserCreate |
| Email | `omitempty,email` | UserCreate |
| Collectible Title | `required,max=300` | CollectibleCreate |
| Collectible Description | `max=5000` | CollectibleCreate |
| Contract Address | `omitempty,max=42` | CollectibleCreate |
| Price (cents) | `required,min=1` | CollectibleCreate |
| Currency | `omitempty,len=3` | CollectibleCreate |
| Reason (cancellation) | `required,max=500` | CancelOrderRequest |
| Message Body | `required,max=5000` | SendMessageRequest |
| CIDR | `required,cidrv4\|cidrv6` | CreateIPRuleRequest |
| Action | `required,oneof=allow deny` | CreateIPRuleRequest |
| Role | `required,oneof=buyer seller administrator compliance_analyst` | AddRoleRequest |
| Traffic % | `required,min=1,max=100` | CreateABTestRequest |

**Server-Side Validation in Handlers:**
- All request validation via `bindAndValidate()` helper
- Message body validation: required + non-empty check
- PII detection for message bodies and attachments
- Attachment MIME type and extension whitelist
- Authorization checks (object-level for orders, role-based for admin endpoints)

**Test Coverage:** `internal/dto/validation_test.go` - comprehensive boundary testing for all DTOs

---

### 6. **Database Schema/Models**

**PostgreSQL 16 with 17 migrations:**

**Core Tables:**

1. **users**
   - `id (UUID PK)`, `username (UNIQUE)`, `password_hash`, `display_name`, `email_encrypted`, `email_hash`
   - `is_locked`, `locked_until`, `failed_login_count`, `created_by (FK)`, timestamps

2. **roles** (4 predefined roles)
   - `buyer`, `seller`, `administrator`, `compliance_analyst`

3. **user_roles** (junction table)
   - `user_id (FK)`, `role_id (FK)`, `granted_by (FK)`, `granted_at`

4. **refresh_tokens**
   - `id (UUID PK)`, `user_id (FK)`, `token_hash`, `family_id`, `expires_at`, `revoked_at`
   - Indexes: `idx_refresh_tokens_user_id`, `idx_refresh_tokens_family_id`

5. **collectibles**
   - `id`, `seller_id (FK)`, `title`, `description`, `price_cents`, `currency`, `status` (draft/published/hidden)
   - `contract_address`, `chain_id`, `token_id`, `metadata_uri`, `image_url`

6. **orders**
   - `id`, `buyer_id (FK)`, `seller_id (FK)`, `collectible_id (FK)`, `status` (pending/confirmed/processing/completed/cancelled)
   - `price_snapshot_cents`, `idempotency_key`, `cancellation_reason`, `cancelled_by (FK)`
   - **Idempotency**: Composite unique constraint `(buyer_id, idempotency_key)`

7. **messages**
   - `id`, `order_id (FK)`, `sender_id (FK)`, `body`, `attachment_path`, `attachment_size`, `attachment_mime`, `created_at`
   - Index: `idx_messages_order (order_id, created_at)`

8. **notifications**
   - `id`, `user_id (FK)`, `template_id (FK)`, `params (JSONB)`, `rendered_title`, `rendered_body`
   - `status` (pending/delivered/failed/permanently_failed), `is_read`, `retry_count`, `max_retries`, `next_retry_at`, `delivered_at`

9. **notification_templates**
   - `id`, `slug (UNIQUE)`, `title_template`, `body_template`, `created_at`

10. **notification_preferences**
    - `user_id (PK)`, `preferences (JSONB)`, `subscription_mode` (all_events/status_only)

11. **analytics_events**
    - `id`, `user_id (FK)`, `event_type`, `collectible_id (FK)`, `session_id`, `ab_variant`, `metadata (JSONB)`, `created_at`

12. **ab_tests**
    - `id`, `name`, `description`, `status`, `traffic_pct`, `start_date`, `end_date`
    - `control_variant`, `test_variant`, `rollback_threshold_pct`, `created_by (FK)`, timestamps

13. **ab_test_results**
    - `id`, `ab_test_id (FK)`, `variant`, `views`, `orders`, `conversion_rate`, `created_at`

14. **audit_logs** (append-only)
    - `id`, `actor_id (FK)`, `action`, `resource_type`, `resource_id`, `details (JSONB)`, `ip_address (INET)`, `created_at`
    - Trigger prevents UPDATE/DELETE; immutable after creation

15. **login_attempts**
    - `id`, `user_id (FK)`, `ip_address (INET)`, `created_at`
    - Rolling-window tracking for lockout detection

16. **ip_rules**
    - `id`, `cidr (CIDR)`, `action` (allow/deny), `created_by (FK)`, `created_at`

17. **order_state_transitions**
    - `id`, `order_id (FK)`, `from_status`, `to_status`, `actor_id (FK)`, `reason`, `created_at`

18. **transaction_history**
    - Immutable record of ownership transfers on order completion

---

### 7. **Authentication/Authorization Patterns**

**Authentication** (`internal/service/auth_service.go`):
- **Login flow**:
  1. IP-based rolling-window check (20 failures in 15 min → lock)
  2. User-level lockout with expiry (5 failures in 15 min → 30 min lockout)
  3. Password verification via bcrypt
  4. Clear failed attempts on success
  5. Issue JWT tokens

**JWT Claims** (`middleware/auth.go`):
```go
type UserClaims struct {
  UserID string   `json:"user_id"`
  Roles  []string `json:"roles"`
  jwt.RegisteredClaims
}
```

**Token Strategy:**
- **Access token**: 15-minute expiry, stored in HttpOnly cookie `access_token`
- **Refresh token**: 7-day expiry, stored in HttpOnly cookie `refresh_token`
- **CSRF token**: 32-byte random hex, stored in non-HttpOnly cookie `csrf_token` (readable by JS)
- Refresh token **family revocation**: Detects token reuse attacks by invalidating entire family

**Authorization** (`middleware/auth.go`):
- Role-based access control via `RequireRole()` middleware
- Valid roles: `buyer`, `seller`, `administrator`, `compliance_analyst`
- Object-level authorization for orders: Only buyer/seller can view/modify
- Object-level authorization for messages: Only order participants can view

**Setup Bootstrap** (`handler/setup_handler.go`):
- First admin must be created via `POST /api/setup/admin`
- Bootstrap password minimum: **12 characters** (stricter than user creation: 8 chars)
- **One-time bootstrap**: Returns 409 Conflict once admin exists
- Setup middleware blocks all API access until complete (except `/api/setup/*`)

**Account Lockout:**
- User level: 5 failed logins in 15 min → locked for 30 min
- IP level: 20 failed logins in 15 min across all users
- Records stored in `login_attempts` table
- `users.is_locked` + `users.locked_until` for time-based expiry

---

### 8. **Retry/Worker Mechanisms**

**Background Workers** (`internal/worker/`):

| Worker | Interval | Purpose | File |
|--------|----------|---------|------|
| **Notification Retry** | 60s | Exponential backoff retry for failed notifications | `notification_retry.go` |
| **Anomaly Detector** | 5min | Excessive cancellations (>6 in 24h), checkout failures (>10 in 1h) | `anomaly_detector.go` |
| **A/B Test Evaluator** | 5min | Computes conversion rates, auto-rollback if threshold exceeded | `abtest_evaluator.go` |
| **Metrics Writer** | 60s | Prometheus-style metrics to log | `metrics_writer.go` |
| **Analytics Rollup** | 1h | Pre-aggregates funnel and retention metrics | `analytics_rollup.go` |
| **Token Cleanup** | 1h | Removes expired refresh tokens | (in main.go) |

**Scheduler** (`worker/scheduler.go`):
- Simple timer-based scheduler
- Each job runs in its own goroutine
- Logs job start/stop with interval
- Context cancellation support

**Notification Retry Logic** (`notification_retry.go`):
1. Get pending notifications (status='pending'), attempt delivery
2. On delivery success → mark 'delivered'
3. On delivery failure → mark 'failed', schedule retry
4. Get failed notifications ready for retry (next_retry_at <= NOW())
5. Exponential backoff: `backoff = 1 << (retryCount + 1) minutes`
6. Max retries configurable; after exhaustion → 'permanently_failed'
7. User can manually retry via `POST /api/notifications/:id/retry`

**Notification State Machine:**
```
pending → delivered (success)
pending → failed (delivery error) → [retry exponential backoff] → delivered/permanently_failed
failed/permanently_failed → [user manual retry] → pending
```

---

### 9. **Logging Patterns**

**Framework:** Zap (structured logging)

**Structured Logger Middleware** (`middleware/logging.go`):
- Captures: method, path, status, latency, remote_ip, user_id, error
- Logs to file with rotation: `/var/log/ledgermint/app.log` (fallback: `./logs/app.log`)
- Log rotation: 100MB per file, 10 backups, 30 days retention
- Compression enabled (gzip)
- Log levels by HTTP status:
  - 5xx → ERROR
  - 4xx → WARN
  - 2xx/3xx → INFO

**Sensitive Field Redaction** (`middleware/logging.go`):
- Redacts: password, token, authorization, cookie, secret

**Request ID** (`middleware/requestid.go`):
- Unique UUID generated per request
- Included in all log entries and error responses
- Allows request tracing through logs

**Audit Logging** (`store/audit_store.go`):
- Append-only table with DB trigger preventing mutations
- Fields: actor_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, details (JSONB), ip_address, created_at
- Best-effort: failures don't block main request path
- Logged for: auth events, admin actions, order transitions, moderation

**Worker Logging** (`worker/scheduler.go`):
- Worker start/stop with interval
- Job failures logged with context

**Example Worker Log:**
```
worker started job=notification_retry interval=1m0s
worker job failed job=anomaly_detector error=<err>
worker stopped job=token_cleanup
```

---

### 10. **Additional Security Features**

**CSRF Protection** (`middleware/csrf.go`):
- Double-submit cookie pattern
- Exempts: GET/HEAD/OPTIONS, `/api/auth/login` (pre-session), `/api/setup/admin` (bootstrap)
- Validates `X-CSRF-Token` header matches `csrf_token` cookie

**IP Filtering** (`middleware/ipfilter.go`):
- CIDR-based allow/deny rules stored in `ip_rules` table
- Cached in-memory for 60 seconds
- Deny rules checked first (default deny)
- Allow rules: if any exist, client must match at least one
- Fail-closed: blocks all traffic if rules cannot be loaded

**Rate Limiting** (`middleware/ratelimit.go`):
- Token bucket algorithm with configurable burst
- Per-endpoint rate limiters:
  - **Login**: 10 per 15 min per IP
  - **Orders**: 30 per min per user, 60 per min per IP
  - **Messages**: 20 per min per user, 40 per min per IP
  - **Listings**: 10 per hour per user, 20 per hour per IP
- Returns `Retry-After: 60` header on throttle
- Automatic cleanup of stale limiters (1-hour cutoff)

**Data Encryption:**
- **Email**: AES-256-GCM encrypted in `users.email_encrypted`
- **Email hash**: Stored separately for lookups
- **Passwords**: bcrypt (cost 12)
- **JWT**: Signed with configurable key (128+ chars required in production)

---

### 11. **Order State Machine**

**Defined Transitions** (`model/order.go`):
```
pending → confirmed → processing → completed
  ↓        ↓
cancelled cancelled
```

**Transition Logic:**
- `pending` → `confirmed` or `cancelled`
- `confirmed` → `processing` or `cancelled`
- `processing` → `completed` (only)
- `completed` and `cancelled` are terminal states

**State Recording:**
- Each transition logged in `order_state_transitions` table
- Records: from_status, to_status, actor_id, reason, timestamp
- Used for analytics and audit trails

**Idempotency:**
- Client provides `Idempotency-Key` header on `POST /api/orders`
- Composite unique key: `(buyer_id, idempotency_key)`
- Duplicate requests return existing order

---

### 12. **Key File Paths Summary**

**Error & Validation:**
- `/backend/internal/dto/errors.go` - Error definitions and codes
- `/backend/internal/dto/request.go` - DTO validation tags
- `/backend/internal/dto/response.go` - Response types
- `/backend/internal/dto/validation_test.go` - Validation tests

**Message Handling:**
- `/backend/internal/handler/message_handler.go` - HTTP endpoints
- `/backend/internal/service/message_service.go` - Business logic
- `/backend/internal/service/pii.go` - PII detection
- `/backend/internal/service/text_extract.go` - Attachment text extraction
- `/backend/internal/store/message_store.go` - Database access

**Authentication & Middleware:**
- `/backend/internal/service/auth_service.go` - Auth logic + lockout
- `/backend/internal/middleware/auth.go` - JWT validation
- `/backend/internal/middleware/setup.go` - Setup guard (setup_required)
- `/backend/internal/middleware/csrf.go` - CSRF protection
- `/backend/internal/middleware/ratelimit.go` - Rate limiting
- `/backend/internal/middleware/ipfilter.go` - IP rules
- `/backend/internal/middleware/logging.go` - Structured logging
- `/backend/internal/middleware/errorhandler.go` - Error normalization

**Database & Models:**
- `/backend/internal/model/order.go` - Order state machine
- `/backend/internal/model/message.go` - Message model
- `/backend/internal/store/` - All store implementations (pgx)
- `/backend/migrations/` - 17 SQL migrations

**Workers:**
- `/backend/internal/worker/scheduler.go` - Job scheduler
- `/backend/internal/worker/notification_retry.go` - Notification retry logic
- `/backend/internal/worker/anomaly_detector.go` - Anomaly detection
- `/backend/internal/worker/abtest_evaluator.go` - A/B test evaluation
- `/backend/internal/worker/metrics_writer.go` - Metrics logging
- `/backend/internal/worker/analytics_rollup.go` - Analytics aggregation

**Entry Point & Router:**
- `/backend/cmd/server/main.go` - Application bootstrap
- `/backend/internal/router/router.go` - Route definitions
- `/backend/internal/cache/hot_cache.go` - In-memory TTL cache

---

This is a production-grade platform with comprehensive security (CSRF, IP filtering, rate limiting, lockout), robust error handling, append-only audit logs, background workers with retry logic, and well-structured layered architecture.

### 2. **Message Handling/Messaging Paths**

**Primary Files:**
- `/backend/internal/handler/message_handler.go` - HTTP handlers
- `/backend/internal/service/message_service.go` - Business logic
- `/backend/internal/store/message_store.go` - Database layer
- `/backend/internal/model/message.go` - Domain model

**Key Features:**
- Multipart form-based message submission with optional attachments
- **10MB attachment size limit** (enforced at handler, service, and middleware levels)
- Server-side **PII detection** for message body and attachments
- Attachment storage: **Filesystem-based** (stored in `/app/uploads/{orderId}/{uuid}{ext}`)
- Attachment path never exposed to client; only opaque message UUID used
- **Attachment types validated**: text (plain/CSV), images (JPEG/PNG/GIF/WebP), PDF, SVG
- Advanced text extraction for PII scanning across all formats:
  - PDF: FlateDecode stream decompression + text operator extraction
  - Binary images: ASCII string extraction from EXIF/metadata
  - SVG/text: scanned as-is
- Database stores: `attachment_path` (filesystem), `attachment_size`, `attachment_mime`
- Authorization: Only order participants (buyer/seller) can access messages
- Rate limiting: **20 per minute per user**, **40 per minute per IP**

**Message Database Schema** (`messages` table):
```sql
id, order_id, sender_id, body, attachment_path, attachment_size, attachment_mime, created_at
```

**API Endpoints:**
- `GET /api/orders/:orderId/messages` - List messages (paginated)
- `POST /api/orders/:orderId/messages` - Send message with attachment
- `GET /api/messages/:messageId/attachment` - Download attachment by message ID

### 3. **Attachment Storage**

**Storage Strategy: Filesystem**
- Path: `/app/uploads/{orderId}/{random-uuid}{original-extension}`
- No database reference for the actual file content
- Database stores only: path, size, MIME type
- Directory created on-demand: `os.MkdirAll(uploadDir, 0o755)`
- File format whitelist enforced both by MIME and extension
- No size tracking in database (calculated from `attachment_size` field)

**PII Detection for Attachments** (`service/text_extract.go`):
- All attachments scanned regardless of format
- Format-specific extraction:
  - **PDF**: Decompresses FlateDecode streams, extracts text from operators `(text) Tj` and `[(text)] TJ`
  - **Binary images**: Extracts contiguous ASCII runs ≥8 chars (catches EXIF, metadata, comments)
  - **SVG/Text**: Scanned as plaintext
- **Regex patterns for PII detection** (`service/pii.go`):
  - SSN: `\b\d{3}-?\d{2}-?\d{4}\b`
  - Phone: `\b(\+?1[-.\s]?)?\(?\d{3}\)?[-.\s]?\d{3}[-.\s]?\d{4}\b`
  - Email: `\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+\-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.\-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b`

ERR_OVERSOLD            // 409
ERR_INTERNAL            // 500
```

- Cached for 2 seconds per request to avoid database hammering

  }
}
```

- Valid roles: `buyer`, `seller`, `administrator`, `compliance_analyst`
- Object-level authorization for orders: Only buyer/seller can view/modify
- Object-level authorization for messages: Only order participants can view

**Setup Bootstrap** (`handler/setup_handler.go`):
- First admin must be created via `POST /api/setup/admin`
- Bootstrap password minimum: **12 characters** (stricter than user creation: 8 chars)
- **One-time bootstrap**: Returns 409 Conflict once admin exists
- Setup middleware blocks all API access until complete (except `/api/setup/*`)

**Account Lockout:**
- User level: 5 failed logins in 15 min → locked for 30 min
- IP level: 20 failed logins in 15 min across all users
- Records stored in `login_attempts` table
- `users.is_locked` + `users.locked_until` for time-based expiry

---

### 8. **Retry/Worker Mechanisms**

**Background Workers** (`internal/worker/`):

| Worker | Interval | Purpose | File |
|--------|----------|---------|------|
| **Notification Retry** | 60s | Exponential backoff retry for failed notifications | `notification_retry.go` |
| **Anomaly Detector** | 5min | Excessive cancellations (>6 in 24h), checkout failures (>10 in 1h) | `anomaly_detector.go` |
| **A/B Test Evaluator** | 5min | Computes conversion rates, auto-rollback if threshold exceeded | `abtest_evaluator.go` |
| **Metrics Writer** | 60s | Prometheus-style metrics to log | `metrics_writer.go` |
| **Analytics Rollup** | 1h | Pre-aggregates funnel and retention metrics | `analytics_rollup.go` |
| **Token Cleanup** | 1h | Removes expired refresh tokens | (in main.go) |

**Scheduler** (`worker/scheduler.go`):
- Simple timer-based scheduler
- Each job runs in its own goroutine
- Logs job start/stop with interval
- Context cancellation support

**Notification Retry Logic** (`notification_retry.go`):
1. Get pending notifications (status='pending'), attempt delivery
2. On delivery success → mark 'delivered'
3. On delivery failure → mark 'failed', schedule retry
4. Get failed notifications ready for retry (next_retry_at <= NOW())
5. Exponential backoff: `backoff = 1 << (retryCount + 1) minutes`
6. Max retries configurable; after exhaustion → 'permanently_failed'
7. User can manually retry via `POST /api/notifications/:id/retry`

**Notification State Machine:**
```
pending → delivered (success)
pending → failed (delivery error) → [retry exponential backoff] → delivered/permanently_failed
failed/permanently_failed → [user manual retry] → pending
```

---

### 9. **Logging Patterns**

**Framework:** Zap (structured logging)

**Structured Logger Middleware** (`middleware/logging.go`):
- Captures: method, path, status, latency, remote_ip, user_id, error
- Logs to file with rotation: `/var/log/ledgermint/app.log` (fallback: `./logs/app.log`)
- Log rotation: 100MB per file, 10 backups, 30 days retention
- Compression enabled (gzip)
- Log levels by HTTP status:
  - 5xx → ERROR
  - 4xx → WARN
  - 2xx/3xx → INFO

**Sensitive Field Redaction** (`middleware/logging.go`):
- Redacts: password, token, authorization, cookie, secret

**Request ID** (`middleware/requestid.go`):
- Unique UUID generated per request
- Included in all log entries and error responses
- Allows request tracing through logs

**Audit Logging** (`store/audit_store.go`):
- Append-only table with DB trigger preventing mutations
- Fields: actor_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, details (JSONB), ip_address, created_at
- Best-effort: failures don't block main request path
- Logged for: auth events, admin actions, order transitions, moderation

**Worker Logging** (`worker/scheduler.go`):
- Worker start/stop with interval
- Job failures logged with context

**Example Worker Log:**
```
worker started job=notification_retry interval=1m0s
worker job failed job=anomaly_detector error=<err>
worker stopped job=token_cleanup
```

---

### 10. **Additional Security Features**

**CSRF Protection** (`middleware/csrf.go`):
- Double-submit cookie pattern
- Exempts: GET/HEAD/OPTIONS, `/api/auth/login` (pre-session), `/api/setup/admin` (bootstrap)
- Validates `X-CSRF-Token` header matches `csrf_token` cookie

**IP Filtering** (`middleware/ipfilter.go`):
- CIDR-based allow/deny rules stored in `ip_rules` table
- Cached in-memory for 60 seconds
- Deny rules checked first (default deny)
- Allow rules: if any exist, client must match at least one
- Fail-closed: blocks all traffic if rules cannot be loaded

**Rate Limiting** (`middleware/ratelimit.go`):
- Token bucket algorithm with configurable burst
- Per-endpoint rate limiters:
  - **Login**: 10 per 15 min per IP
  - **Orders**: 30 per min per user, 60 per min per IP
  - **Messages**: 20 per min per user, 40 per min per IP
  - **Listings**: 10 per hour per user, 20 per hour per IP
- Returns `Retry-After: 60` header on throttle
- Automatic cleanup of stale limiters (1-hour cutoff)

**Data Encryption:**
- **Email**: AES-256-GCM encrypted in `users.email_encrypted`
- **Email hash**: Stored separately for lookups
- **Passwords**: bcrypt (cost 12)
- **JWT**: Signed with configurable key (128+ chars required in production)

---

### 11. **Order State Machine**

**Defined Transitions** (`model/order.go`):
```
pending → confirmed → processing → completed
  ↓        ↓
cancelled cancelled
```

**Transition Logic:**
- `pending` → `confirmed` or `cancelled`
- `confirmed` → `processing` or `cancelled`
- `processing` → `completed` (only)
- `completed` and `cancelled` are terminal states

**State Recording:**
- Each transition logged in `order_state_transitions` table
- Records: from_status, to_status, actor_id, reason, timestamp
- Used for analytics and audit trails

**Idempotency:**
- Client provides `Idempotency-Key` header on `POST /api/orders`
- Composite unique key: `(buyer_id, idempotency_key)`
- Duplicate requests return existing order

---

### 12. **Key File Paths Summary**

**Error & Validation:**
- `/backend/internal/dto/errors.go` - Error definitions and codes
- `/backend/internal/dto/request.go` - DTO validation tags
- `/backend/internal/dto/response.go` - Response types

**Database & Models:**

13	// RequireSetup returns middleware that blocks all API requests (except the
14	// setup endpoints themselves) until at least one administrator account exists.
15	// Once an admin is detected the check is cached so it does not hit the
16	// database on every request.
17	func RequireSetup(userStore *store.UserStore) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
18		var (
19			mu       sync.RWMutex
20			complete bool
21			checked  time.Time
22		)
23	
24		isComplete := func(ctx context.Context) bool {
25			mu.RLock()
26			if complete {
27				mu.RUnlock()
28				return true
29			}
30			// Re-check at most once every 2 seconds to avoid hammering the DB.
31			stale := time.Since(checked) > 2*time.Second
32			mu.RUnlock()
33	
34			if !stale {
35				return false
36			}
37	
38			exists, err := userStore.AdminExists(ctx)
39			if err != nil {
40				return false
41			}
42	
43			mu.Lock()
44			checked = time.Now()
45			if exists {
46				complete = true
47			}
48			mu.Unlock()
49			return exists
50		}
51	
52		return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
53			return func(c echo.Context) error {
54				if isComplete(c.Request().Context()) {
55					return next(c)
56				}
57				return c.JSON(http.StatusServiceUnavailable, map[string]interface{}{
58					"error": map[string]string{
59						"code":    "setup_required",
60						"message": "Initial setup required. Create an administrator account via POST /api/setup/admin before using the API.",
61					},
62				})
63			}
64		}
65	}

12	func GlobalErrorHandler(err error, c echo.Context) {
13		if c.Response().Committed {
14			return
15		}
16	
17		code := http.StatusInternalServerError
18		errCode := dto.CodeInternal
19		message := "internal server error"
20	
21		if he, ok := err.(*echo.HTTPError); ok {
22			code = he.Code
23	
24			// If the error message is already our ErrorResponse format, use it directly
25			if resp, ok := he.Message.(dto.ErrorResponse); ok {
26				c.JSON(code, resp)
27				return
28			}
29	
30			// Map common HTTP status codes to our error codes
31			switch code {
32			case http.StatusBadRequest:
33				errCode = dto.CodeValidation
34				message = "bad request"
35			case http.StatusUnauthorized:
36				errCode = dto.CodeUnauthorized
37				message = "unauthorized"
38			case http.StatusForbidden:
39				errCode = dto.CodeForbidden
40				message = "forbidden"
41			case http.StatusNotFound:
42				errCode = dto.CodeNotFound
43				message = "not found"
44			case http.StatusMethodNotAllowed:
45				errCode = dto.CodeValidation
46				message = "method not allowed"
47			case http.StatusTooManyRequests:
48				errCode = dto.CodeRateLimited
49				message = "rate limited"
50			case http.StatusServiceUnavailable:
51				errCode = dto.CodeInternal
52				message = "service unavailable"
53			}
54	
55			// Use the echo error message if it's a string
56			if msg, ok := he.Message.(string); ok && msg != "" {

58			}
59		}
60	
61		c.JSON(code, dto.ErrorResponse{
62			Error: dto.ErrorDetail{
63				Code:      errCode,

66			},

14	// RequireSetup returns middleware that blocks all API requests (except the
15	// setup endpoints themselves) until at least one administrator account exists.
16	// Once an admin is detected the check is cached so it does not hit the
17	// database on every request.
18	func RequireSetup(userStore *store.UserStore) echo.MiddlewareFunc {
19		var (
20			mu       sync.RWMutex
21			complete bool
22			checked  time.Time
23		)
24	
25		isComplete := func(ctx context.Context) bool {
26			mu.RLock()
27			if complete {
28				mu.RUnlock()
29				return true
30			}
31			// Re-check at most once every 2 seconds to avoid hammering the DB.
32			stale := time.Since(checked) > 2*time.Second
33			mu.RUnlock()
34	
35			if !stale {
36				return false
37			}
38	
39			exists, err := userStore.AdminExists(ctx)
40			if err != nil {
41				return false
42			}
43	
44			mu.Lock()
45			checked = time.Now()
46			if exists {
47				complete = true
48			}
49			mu.Unlock()
50			return exists
51		}
52	
53		return func(next echo.HandlerFunc) echo.HandlerFunc {
54			return func(c echo.Context) error {
55				if isComplete(c.Request().Context()) {
56					return next(c)
57				}
58				return c.JSON(http.StatusServiceUnavailable, dto.ErrorResponse{
59					Error: dto.ErrorDetail{
60						Code:      dto.CodeSetupRequired,
61						Message:   "Initial setup required. Create an administrator account via POST /api/setup/admin before using the API.",
62						RequestID: GetRequestID(c),
63					},
64				})
65			}
66		}
67	}
